message:
fix(snapshot): verify state_hash on load (ft-qf3vk)

ft-ybtyg (598a7c13) replaced the hardcoded 'restore' literal with
a real SipHash via compute_restore_state_hash and documented the
intent that 'a later integrity check can recompute and compare'.
The write half landed; the read half did not. session_checkpoints
rows now carry useful-looking SipHash values but load_checkpoint_by_id
never recomputed and compared them, so a tampered row (flipped hash,
flipped pane_id_map, flipped checkpoint_at, etc.) was silently
restored exactly as before.

Close the gap:

- Add RestoreError::StateHashMismatch with the checkpoint id,
  session id, stored hash, and recomputed hash for forensics.
- load_checkpoint_by_id now SELECTs state_hash and metadata_json
  alongside the existing fields.
- For checkpoint_type = 'startup' rows (the ones produced by
  finalize_restore / save_restore_checkpoint), call
  verify_restore_state_hash before returning the checkpoint.
- verify_restore_state_hash reconstructs the pane_id_map from
  metadata_json.old_to_new (which both INSERT sites write), reruns
  compute_restore_state_hash over (session_id, pane_id_map,
  checkpoint_at_raw), and returns StateHashMismatch on any
  disagreement. Missing/malformed metadata_json is treated as a
  mismatch (can't prove integrity → refuse to restore).
- checkpoint_type = 'periodic' rows are NOT verified: they are
  written by snapshot_engine::compute_state_hash over PaneInfo
  inputs that are not reconstructable from session_checkpoints
  alone. That remains a documented follow-up; the current fix
  only reverses the ft-ybtyg trust-gap on startup rows.

Regression tests (5):
- load_checkpoint_by_id_rejects_tampered_state_hash: flip hash col
- load_checkpoint_by_id_rejects_tampered_pane_id_mapping: flip
  metadata_json.old_to_new without touching the hash
- load_checkpoint_by_id_rejects_legacy_restore_literal: pre-ybtyg
  rows with state_hash='restore' must NOT pass verification
- load_checkpoint_by_id_rejects_missing_metadata_on_startup_row:
  NULL metadata_json is itself a tampering signal, not a pass
- load_checkpoint_by_id_skips_verification_for_non_startup_checkpoints:
  guard against regressing 'periodic' rows (no false positives)

Authored by the previous agent (P1) before hitting a usage limit;
reviewed for completeness and committed as ft-qf3vk handoff.
Local compile verification was blocked by rch force_remote=true
against unavailable workers (cargo → SIGTERM); the change uses
only existing imports (rusqlite::params inside the test module,
serde_json via fully-qualified paths) and existing helpers
(finalize_restore, insert_checkpoint, setup_test_db, insert_session),
and does not touch the broken *_with_cx surfaces that are
producing pre-existing build errors in concurrent agents' work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
